steps.title: Pinpoint what outcome would prove the claim
steps.content: The researcher claims that eliminating left turns improves overall travel efficiency despite longer routes. This would be proven by data showing improved throughput after elimination.

steps.title: Match the correct answer to this outcome
steps.content: Choice A shows exactly this—drivers reach more addresses daily after elimination, proving overall efficiency improved despite longer individual routes. The others test signals rather than elimination, measure only individual left-turn wait times, or show routes took longer. That matches choice A — A is correct.
